seoTitle: How to Connect Canva to Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Install the Canva add-on for Google Docs so you can browse your designs and insert live-updating graphics directly into your documents.
intro: Embedding polished Canva designs into a Google Doc shouldn't mean downloading images and re-uploading them manually. This guide walks through installing the Canva extension for Google Docs and inserting designs straight from your sidebar.

steps.heading: Access the Google Workspace Marketplace
steps.body: In your document, click Extensions, hover over Add-ons, and select Get Add-ons to browse available integrations.

steps.heading: Install the Canva extension
steps.body: Search for Canva, choose the official listing, and click Install, then approve the requested workspace permissions.

steps.heading: Sign in to your Canva account
steps.body: Follow the redirect prompt to log into your Canva account and complete the connection between the two platforms.

steps.heading: Browse and insert a design
steps.body: Open the Canva panel from the right sidebar in Google Docs, select a design from your folders, and click Insert to place it on the page.

steps.heading: Check for live updates
steps.body: Edit the original design in Canva and confirm the embedded graphic in your document reflects the change.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Canva plan to use this integration?
faq.answer: A free Canva account works for basic design insertion, though some premium templates require a paid plan.

faq.question: Can multiple team members insert designs from the same Canva account?
faq.answer: Each person needs their own connected Canva account, but shared team folders can still be accessed if permissions allow.

faq.question: Will resizing the image in Google Docs affect the original design?
faq.answer: No, resizing in Google Docs only changes how it displays in your document, not the source file in Canva.

faq.question: What if the design doesn't update automatically?
faq.answer: Try removing and re-inserting the image, or check that you're still signed into the same connected Canva account.
